skill.name: pluto-pair
skill.description: Guide Codex, Claude, and Claude Code through Pluto.jl notebook workflows using MCP tools for live sessions, headless checks, controls, state review, and HTML exports.
skill.summary: Use MCP tools to inspect, control, validate, and export Pluto.jl notebooks.
skill.author: EzraCerpac

security_audit.summary: The Ruby or shell backtick findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline tool names, not executable code. The weak cryptography finding on the description line is also a false positive, and no prompt injection or data exfiltration evidence was found. The real concern is that the skill directs agents to open and execute trusted Pluto.jl notebooks by default, so untrusted notebooks require preview mode.

medium_findings.title: Notebook Execution Requires Trust Boundary
medium_findings.description: The skill tells agents to open and execute trusted Pluto.jl notebooks, with execution enabled by default. This is legitimate for notebook automation, but unsafe for untrusted notebooks because notebook code can run during execution.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 21 explicitly states that opening a notebook executes it by default and limits disabled execution to untrusted preview. The context is legitimate Pluto automation, so the concern is trust-boundary handling rather than confirmed malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Tool Names Flagged as Shell Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command detections are Markdown inline code spans naming MCP tools and workflow terms. No evidence found of Ruby backtick execution or shell command construction in the reviewed skill file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown documentation bullets or prose using inline code formatting. There is no Ruby source, shell interpolation, or executable backtick context in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Description
low_findings.description: The reported blocker on the description line does not correspond to a weak cryptographic algorithm. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic operation on that line.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is metadata text describing Pluto.jl notebook MCP workflows. The static match has no supporting semantic evidence of cryptography or malicious behavior.

low_findings.title: HTML Export Writes Files
low_findings.description: The skill includes an export workflow that writes rendered HTML. This is expected functionality, but users should choose output locations carefully and avoid overwriting important files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 25 clearly says the export tool writes rendered HTML. The risk is limited because the skill only documents tool use and does not show arbitrary file writes.

dangerous_patterns.title: Default Notebook Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented workflow executes trusted notebooks by default. Agents should use disabled execution for untrusted previews.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The execution behavior is directly documented on the cited line. The skill also provides a mitigation for untrusted notebooks, reducing malicious-confidence but not operational risk.

content.value_statement: Pluto.jl notebook work can be hard to inspect from an AI coding session. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a clear workflow for attaching, controlling, reading, and exporting notebooks.
actual_capabilities: Discover visible local Pluto.jl servers before attaching to a session.
Attach to an existing notebook that the user has open in a browser.
Open notebooks in visible or headless Pluto sessions when appropriate.
Inspect @bind controls and set bond values to trigger reactive updates.
Read compact notebook state, including code, outputs, errors, logs, and bonds.
Export rendered notebook output as HTML.
limitations: It depends on a compatible pluto-pair MCP server being available.
It does not provide the MCP server implementation inside this skill file.
Notebook execution should be limited to trusted notebooks unless preview mode is used.
Browser automation is only recommended for screenshots and visual smoke checks.

use_cases.title: Debug Reactive Notebook State
use_cases.description: Inspect controls, outputs, errors, and logs from a running Pluto.jl notebook.

use_cases.title: Validate Notebook Changes Headlessly
use_cases.description: Open a trusted notebook in a headless session and review execution state before sharing.

use_cases.title: Create Shareable HTML Exports
use_cases.description: Render a Pluto notebook and export HTML for review or publication workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Attach to My Notebook
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for an already open Pluto notebook.
prompt_templates.prompt: Find my visible Pluto.jl server, attach to the open notebook, and summarize its current state.

prompt_templates.title: Inspect Notebook Controls
prompt_templates.scenario: Review interactive controls before changing values.
prompt_templates.prompt: Attach to my Pluto.jl notebook, list the @bind controls, and explain what each control appears to affect.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Trusted Notebook Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Headless validation for a notebook you trust.
prompt_templates.prompt: Open this trusted Pluto.jl notebook headlessly, let it execute, then report errors, logs, outputs, and bond values.

prompt_templates.title: Tune Controls and Export
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow that changes reactive state and exports output.
prompt_templates.prompt: Attach to the Pluto.jl notebook, update the requested bond values, verify the reactive outputs, and export the final view as HTML.

output_examples.input: Attach to my visible Pluto notebook and summarize it.
output_examples.output: Found one visible Pluto server, attached to the active notebook, and summarized cells, outputs, errors, logs, and available controls.

output_examples.input: Change the slider values and check the result.
output_examples.output: Listed the bound controls, updated the requested values, then reported the changed outputs and any new errors.
Opened the trusted notebook, confirmed the rendered state, and produced an HTML export for review.

best_practices: Use live attach first when the user already has a Pluto browser window open.
Use disabled execution for untrusted notebooks or static previews.
Close or detach notebook sessions when the task is finished.
anti_patterns: Do not execute notebooks from unknown sources without explicit trust.
Do not use browser automation when MCP state tools provide the needed information.
Do not export HTML before checking notebook errors and reactive output state.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides AI agents through Pluto.jl notebook workflows using pluto-pair MCP tools.

faq.question: Does it work with an open browser notebook?
faq.answer: Yes. It prefers discovering a visible Pluto server and attaching to the notebook the user already sees.

faq.question: Can it run notebooks without a browser?
faq.answer: Yes. It describes headless notebook opening for trusted checks, validation, exports, and static previews.

faq.question: Can it change Pluto @bind controls?
faq.answer: Yes. It can inspect bound variables and set bond values through the MCP workflow.

faq.question: Is it safe for untrusted notebooks?
faq.answer: Use preview mode with execution disabled for untrusted notebooks. Trusted notebooks may execute by default.

faq.question: Does the skill include the MCP server?
faq.answer: No. It provides usage guidance and requires the pluto-pair MCP server to be available.
